this still ManChicken
not mocking btw, but commiserating

Let me give you some context, in cleste, there is tech called super dash, the comunity shortened it to super, now, by this nameing scheme, there is tech that are named 'hyper' 'ultra' and 'wavedash', and also, if you do multiple ultras after each other, its called chained ultras

Super and other types of dash tech, where you dash in a certain way and then jump to do a lot of momentum, the different dashes (supers, wavedashes, ultras, hypers) are mostly just the different ways you dash into a jump, but you can also do a thing, where you dash to the ground in a certain direction, but then swap where you are moving and jump, it will in most cases still give you this momentum, but to the other direction, this is what we call reverse dashes
So reverse super, reverse wavedash, ect.
So, we can finaly move onto the joke I wated to make with this explained
The existance of ultrakill implies the existance of  chained ultrakills, reverse ultrakills, superkills and hyperkills
Maybe even wavekills
Oh, and fuper is feather superdash, basically super out of a feather
However cassette bal

Idk if you seen the fuper explanation message, but casseted boosted needs a little explanation too

Cassete blocks are rythm blocks, they appear and dissapear based on the rhythm, but, this is a bit more complex, because when the cassette block reapears, it does like a pop or something, like the first frame of cassette reapearing, if you jump it will give you a momentum, this is what we call cassette boosting
Basically it gives you jump boost the frame it starts existing
Thank you for listening to my celeste tech lession, I will see you next year

The question: "Putting `` inside `` is okay, right?"
The answer: "A span is an inline element, and must only contain text content or nested inline or phrase elements. It shouldn't be used to surround block-level elements."

The spec, on what content `<span>` allows: "If the element is a descendant of an `option` element: Zero or more `option` element inner content elements, except `div` elements. Otherwise: *Phrasing content*."
The spec, on what `` is: "Flow content. *Phrasing content*. Embedded content. Form-associated element. If the element has a `usemap` attribute: Interactive content. Palpable content."
Guys I think someone is wrong on the Internet
